palette_hint: Deep navy #0a1628 background, off-white #e8f4f8 body text, electric teal #00c9b1 and coral #ff6b47 as accent colors
type_style: Georgia serif for display headings (bold 700), system-ui sans-serif for body and data labels (400/600), clean scientific feel with generous line-height 1.6
description: Deep Current is an interactive infographic site presenting peer-reviewed ocean health data through dense SVG charts, illustrated cross-sections, and multi-column data layouts aimed at climate researchers and educators.
constraints: Max content width 1280px centered, with a persistent 240px left sidebar on all pages containing a sticky SVG wave logo, section nav links, and a live depth-scale ruler illustration; main content area uses remaining width in a 3-column grid collapsing to 2 at 900px

Every page must contain at least 3 distinct SVG figures: one decorative organic-shape divider (wavy path between sections), one data visualization (bar/radial/scatter chart built entirely from SVG primitives), and one illustrative icon cluster (minimum 6 icons per cluster built from path and circle elements)

Accent color #00c9b1 is used exclusively for positive/healthy data indicators and CTA buttons; coral #ff6b47 is used exclusively for warning/crisis data indicators and alert callout borders; never mix the two on the same chart
All section headers use a clip-path polygon cut on a teal-to-navy gradient banner (skewed 3deg), minimum 48px font-size, with a drop-shadow filter of 0 4px 24px rgba(0,201,177,0.3)

Data cards are 280px wide, border-radius 16px, background #0f2240, box-shadow 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.4), padding 24px, each containing an inline SVG icon (min 8 primitives), a large numeric stat at 56px font-size in teal, and 2-3 lines of explanatory text at 14px

page_specs.ocean-health: The homepage presents a full-width hero featuring a layered SVG ocean cross-section illustration showing five depth zones (sunlight 0-200m, twilight 200-1000m, midnight 1000-4000m, abyssal 4000-6000m, hadal 6000m+) each colored with SVG gradient fills and populated with 8-12 SVG creature silhouettes per zone. Below the hero, a 3-column stat dashboard displays 9 data cards covering pH levels (8.1→7.9 since 1850), dissolved oxygen percentage, coral coverage loss (50% since 1950), sea level rise (3.3mm/yr), Arctic ice extent, fish biomass index, phytoplankton density, microplastic concentration, and ocean heat content in zettajoules. A wavy SVG divider in teal separates the hero from the dashboard.
page_specs.species-atlas: A two-panel layout with the left 60% showing a stylized SVG world map built from polygon continent shapes with 47 circular hotspot markers (radius scaled to biodiversity index, colored teal for thriving to coral for endangered) connected by curved SVG path migration routes. The right 40% sidebar lists 12 featured species cards each containing a hand-drawn SVG portrait (min 15 path primitives per portrait) of the animal, conservation status badge, population trend sparkline built from SVG polyline, and three data rows for range, diet, and primary threat. A radial SVG chart at the bottom of the page shows taxonomic distribution across 8 marine phyla using concentric ring segments.
page_specs.temperature-rise: The primary visualization is a large 900px-wide SVG area chart showing sea surface temperature anomalies from 1850 to 2024, with the baseline period 1951-1980 marked by a dashed reference line, the area above filled coral #ff6b47 at 60% opacity and the area below filled teal #00c9b1 at 40% opacity, and 12 labeled event markers (El Niño years, volcanic eruptions) as SVG circle+text annotations. A secondary 3-column section below presents regional breakdowns for Arctic, Tropical Pacific, and Southern Ocean using individual SVG line charts (each 260px wide, 160px tall) with grid lines, axis labels, and a trend arrow polygon. An SVG thermometer illustration on the right sidebar animates its fill height via CSS height on a clipPath rect, showing current +1.2°C anomaly.
page_specs.plastic-crisis: A bold infographic page dominated by a central SVG pipeline/flow diagram showing the lifecycle of ocean plastic from land sources (rivers, coastlines, shipping) through five ocean garbage patches to seafloor accumulation, with each flow represented as curved SVG path arrows whose stroke-width is proportional to tonnage (8M tons/yr total). Flanking the diagram are two vertical stat columns: left column covers production stats (380M tons plastic produced annually, 8M entering ocean daily) and right column covers impact stats (1M seabirds killed/yr, 100K marine mammals, 700 species affected). At the bottom a stacked horizontal SVG bar chart breaks down plastic types by percentage (PET 26%, HDPE 18%, PVC 10%, LDPE 16%, PP 21%, PS 6%, other 3%) with each bar segment labeled and color-coded using 7 distinct fills.
page_specs.take-action: A multi-section action page opening with an SVG illustrated banner showing a stylized research vessel with 20+ path-built detail elements (hull, mast, instruments, wake waves) against a sunrise gradient sky. Below, a 2-column layout presents 6 pledge cards on the left (reduce single-use plastic, support MPAs, eat sustainable seafood, offset carbon, donate to reef restoration, join citizen science) each with a unique SVG icon badge (min 10 primitives each) and a teal CTA button. The right column contains an SVG radial progress chart showing global MPA coverage progress toward the 30x30 target (currently 8% of 30% goal), a vertical timeline SVG diagram marking 8 key ocean protection milestones from 1972 MARPOL to 2023 High Seas Treaty, and a contact form styled with teal focus-ring borders and coral required-field indicators.
